title: Coastal and beach richness phase
summary: Fifty-third structured packet for Eco Explorer. This second lane-2 long-push wave rebalances the front half of the world through one beach and coastal-scrub richness pack and then one compact front-half memory payoff.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the beach and coastal-scrub richness pack.
execution_order.why_now: The front half now wants a comparable amount of authored delight.
A richer opening chain will make the full world feel more balanced.
The pack needs to stay science-safe and readable in the first twenty minutes.
Once the content lands, one compact payoff can make those early spaces more memorable.
The beach and scrub should feel as worth remembering as the inland spaces.
The payoff should stay compact and calm.

guardrails: Avoid reopening corridor or route runtime.
Prefer a few strong front-half anchors over a broad taxonomy push.
Keep the payoff inside an existing surface.
Target the lighter front-half zones first: beach dry-sand and the coastal-scrub shore-pine stand.
Treat the memory payoff as a sketchbook-first pass instead of broadening comparison or close-look surfaces.
acceptance_focus: The opening biomes feel denser and more delightful.
The front half gains clearer upper-dune and shore-pine underlayer anchors instead of more tide-line or thicket clutter.
One compact sketchbook-first payoff makes the front half more memorable.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-2.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-coastal-and-beach-richness-phase.md, and this packet. Treat this as the second lane-2 long-push wave: deepen beach and coastal scrub through one compact richness pack and one small memory payoff without reopening route or traversal systems.
